rulings.Case Title: DEBORA ANDERSON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is granted in its entirety. Plaintiff has failed to provide evidence that Defendant had actual or constructive notice of the dangerous condition a sufficient time prior to the time of the accident so that measure could have been taken to protect against the dangerous condition. A public entity is not liable for a dangerous condition where there is no proof either that a public employee created the condition or that the public entity had actual or constructive knowledge thereof. Van Kempen v. Hayward Area Park, etc., Dist. (1972), 23 Cal. App. 3d 822, 825. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JP BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. LISA LEEBOVE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Pltf'S Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Its First Set Of Form Interrogatories To Deft Lisa Leebove And Steve Steinberg And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2013, Line 26, PLAINTIFF JP BUILDERS INC.'s motion To Compel Further Responses To Its First Set Of Form Interrogatories To Defendant Lisa Leebove And Steve Steinberg And For Monetary Sanctions. Granted in part as to Form Interrogatories nos. 2.1, 2.2, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 2.8, 2.9, 2.10, 2.11, 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 3.6, 3.7, 4.1, 4.2, 7.1, 7.2, 7.3, 9.1, 9.2, 1.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.6, 12.7, 14.1, 14.2, 15.1, 17.1, and 50.1. Based on the meet and confer, the term "INCIDENT" is not vague or ambiguous. Defendants must specifically respond to each interrogatory and not provide identical answers, where on its face, the response cannot have the same answer. (Compare Form Interrogatory no. 2.1, with Form Interrogatory no. 2.2.) Further responses to Form Interrogatories no. 17.1 shall be limited to Request for Admission nos. 1, 2, 3, 5, 16, 18, 19, 21, 21.5, 22, 23, 31, 32, 44, 45, and 49. For Form Interrogatories no. 50.1, Defendants shall identify the documents per CCP § 2030.230. For Form Interrogatories no. 50.2, Defendants shall provide the date of every act or omission that are claimed as a breach of the agreement. Denied as to Form Interrogatories nos. 50.3, 50.4, and 50.5 as the responses are sufficient. Defendants shall serve further responses no later than February 1, 2013. Sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JP BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. LISA LEEBOVE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Pltf'S Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Its Second Set Of Inspection Demands To Deft Lisa Leebove And Steve Steinberg And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2013, Line 26, PLAINTIFF JP BUILDERS INC.'s motion To Compel Further Responses To Its Second Set Of Inspection Demands To Defendant Lisa Leebove And Steve Steinberg And For Monetary Sanctions. Granted. Defendants' objections lack merit. The requests adequately describes what it seeks, and it is not too onerous to ask the responding party to produce documents the party identified in response to interrogatories. Monetary sanctions of $ 1,400.00 are awarded to Plaintiff. Defendants shall serve further responses no later than February 1, 2013 and pay the monetary sanctions no later than February 14, 2013. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JP BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. LISA LEEBOVE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Pltf'S Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Its First Set Of Requests For Admission To Deft Lisa Leebove And Steve Steinberg And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2013, Line 26, PLAINTIFF JP BUILDERS INC.'s motion To Compel Further Responses To Its First Set Of Requests For Admission To Defendant Lisa Leebove And Steve Steinberg And For Monetary Sanctions. Granted as to Request For Admission nos. 1, 2, 3, 5, 16, 18, 19, 21, 21.5, 22, 23, 31, 32, 44, 45, and 49. None of the requests for admissions are so vague and ambiguous as to preclude an intelligent reply. Denied as to Requests nos. 24 and 25. The use of the phrase "a transaction" in render these ambiguous as to preclude an intelligent reply. Denied as to Request nos. 35, 36, 37, 38, 40, 41, 42, and 43. Although requests for admission may ask a party to admit the application of law to fact, see C.C.P. § 2033.010, these requests for admission, as phrased, seek an "admission" of a proposition of law rather than the application of law to facts in this case. Defendants shall serve further responses no later than February 1, 2013. Sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PERFORMING ARTS LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. MICHAEL MURRAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendants Leo Cassidy, James Nunemacher, Deirdre Cassidy, Lj Sf Investments Llc'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2013, Line 23, Cross Defendants Leo Cassidy, James Nunemacher, Deirdre Cassidy, Lj Sf Investments Llc'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Cross Complaint. Denied as to for punitive damages and attorney fees and granted as to injunctive relief. The prayer for punitive damages and attorney fees are not improper. Cross-Defendants jointly and not severally moved to strike the allegations, and Cross-Defendants admit that there is an attorney fee provision in 973 Market Street's contract with Cross-Complainant. Cross-Defendants rely on extrinsic evidence, which is improper. The injunctive relief sought is improper. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW FRAZER VS. ROMAN CATHOLIC BISHOP OF SAN JOSE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Diocese Of Fresno Education Corporation, The Roman Catholic Bishop Of Fresno, A Corporation'S Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 14, 2013, Line 5, Defendants Diocese Of Fresno Education Corporation, The Roman Catholic Bishop Of Fresno's Motion For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. While the Court is sensitive to Monsignor Minhoto¿s (¿Minhoto¿) health concerns, the Court is persuaded that this deposition should proceed subject to the limitations offered by Plaintiff¿s counsel ¿ i.e., the deposition will be limited to one hour, it will take place at Minhoto¿s assisted living facility or whatever other location is most convenient for him, Minhoto will be permitted to take any breaks he needs during the deposition, and counsel shall be polite and respectful to Minhoto at all times during the deposition. The Court is persuaded that Minhoto¿s testimony is potentially probative of the issue of defendant Diocese of Fresno Education Corporation¿s knowledge of the risk allegedly posed by Father Flickinger, and Defendants make no attempt to argue to the contrary. Plaintiff has also argued ¿ and Defendants do not dispute -- that Minhoto¿s testimony may have additional relevance in that he later had involvement in employment decisions regarding Father Flickinger as Chancellor. Dr. Tetrud¿s declaration does not state whether his conclusions would apply to a deposition conducted under the limitations offered by Plaintiff¿s counsel, and faulty memory is always a concern in litigation in any case. And there is at least some suggestion from Monsignor Moreton and Defense counsel that Minhoto is capable of giving deposition testimony. For the foregoing reasons, the Court is not persuaded that ¿justice requires¿ an order that this deposition not be taken, nor is the Court persuaded that the burdens clearly outweigh the benefits sought to be obtained from the deposition. See CCP §§ 2025.420(b) and 2017.020(a). Accordingly, the motion for protective order is denied, and Minhoto¿s deposition shall proceed subject to the limitations set forth above. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 14, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF NETLINE CORPORATION'S, Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Interrogatories, And For Order That The Truth Of The Matter Specified In Request's For Admission Be Deemed Admitted, For Reasonable Attorney's Fees, Costs, And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT CAROLYN BOLANOS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Off calendar per January 3 dismissal. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/EHG)
